title: maranta tessellata var. kegeljanii
intro: Maranta tessellata var. kegeljanii, also known as the Kegeljan's Maranta, is a variety of the Maranta tessellata plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Maranta tessellata var. kegeljanii include relief from coughs, colds, and flu, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory ailments and promote overall well-being.
